data.title: Senate Banking Committee postpones markup of crucial crypto bill due to dwindling industry backing.Character count: 87
data.description: Efforts in the Senate to revise U.S. cryptocurrency regulations faced a setback when the Banking Committee postponed the markup of the significant bill.

data.title: Peter Schiff warns against selling gold and silver for Bitcoin, calling it a mistake. Bitcoin rally doubted.
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff has expressed skepticism about the current surge in Bitcoin's value, suggesting that investors are shifting their profits from commodities to cryptocurrency-related assets. Schiff believes that traders are making a significant error by selling off gold and silver mining stocks to invest in Bitcoin exchange-traded funds and Strategy. He recommends that investors reconsider this strategy and instead sell off their Bitcoin and MSTR holdings to purchase stocks linked to precious metals. Earlier this month, Schiff had already cautioned against viewing Bitcoin's rise as a positive indicator, dismissing it as merely an inflated marketing narrative. Looking ahead to 2026, it is expected that both cryptocurrency and precious metal markets will continue to experience growth.
China has approved Myqorzo and Redemplo. More details: Myqorzo helps 300 million patients, Redemplo benefits 150 million.
Swissmedic approves AGAMREE® (Vamorolone) for treating Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy by Santhera.

data.title: TGS announces APEX 1, a new OBN Multi-client Survey in the Gulf of America.
data.description: TGS has announced the launch of APEX 1, a pioneering multi-client long-offset Ocean Bottom Node (OBN) acquisition campaign in the Gulf of America. This cutting-edge project marks a significant advancement in offshore seismic data collection and is set to cover a vast area in the Gulf. It represents a breakthrough in technology and strategy, setting new industry standards. APEX 1 is expected to cover an extensive area of X square kilometers and involve an investment of approximately $Y million. It signifies TGS's commitment to innovation and excellence in the exploration sector, showcasing their dedication to providing high-quality data solutions for clients in the offshore energy industry.

data.title: Ascom reports 2025 revenue of CHF 292.1 million, up 3.8% at constant currencies, with EBITDA margin of 11.5-12%.
data.description: As of January 15, 2026, in a statement made in Baar, Switzerland, Ascom disclosed that their net revenue for the fiscal year 2025 amounted to CHF 292.1 million based on early unaudited data. This marks a growth of approximately 3.8% when adjusted for currency fluctuations. Additionally, the company achieved an EBITDA margin ranging between 11.5% to 12.0%.
Richemont's Q3 sales surge by 11% at constant rates, ending on 31 December 2025, continuing strong momentum.

data.title: Midnight Foundation partners with Bloomsbury Money for FX, cross-border transfers, and e-money solutions.
data.description: In London on January 15, 2026, The Midnight Foundation announced an important partnership with Bloomsbury Money for foreign exchange, cross-border payments, and employee debit cards. This exclusive partnership signifies a significant step for both parties, connecting The Midnight Foundation's Privacy Enhanced blockchain world with Bloomsbury Money's services. This collaboration is set to be a long-term alliance, solidifying their commitment to enhancing privacy and efficiency in financial transactions.

data.title: ChainUp named among Singapore's top Fintech Companies 2026 by Statista and Tech in Asia, a first-time ranking collaboration.
data.description: ChainUp, a renowned digital asset technology company based in Singapore, was recently recognized as one of the top fintech companies in Singapore for 2026. This accolade was awarded by Tech in Asia, a leading tech media platform in the region. The list, which is in its inaugural year, showcases the most innovative and influential companies in the fintech sector. This recognition underscores ChainUp's prominent position in the industry and its significant contributions to the digital asset landscape.

data.title: TetraScience appoints Matt Studney as Chief Customer Officer, a 24-year Merck vet, to industrialize scientific data and AI in biopharma.
data.description: A veteran of 24 years at Merck and former Senior Vice President of Research and Development Information Technology, Matt Studney has joined TetraScience in Boston on January 15, 2026. He will serve as the Chief Customer Officer to assist in the industrialization of scientific data and artificial intelligence (AI) within the biopharmaceutical industry. TetraScience aims to enhance data management and AI utilization across biopharma, with Studney bringing his extensive experience to the role.

data.title: CapaSystems launches CapaOne, a cloud-based platform to enhance Microsoft Intune. Offers consolidated endpoint operations.
data.description: Copenhagen, Denmark - On January 15, 2026, CapaSystems A/S, a prominent European company with over 30 years of expertise in endpoint management and cybersecurity advancements, introduced a new platform designed to streamline endpoint operations for contemporary IT teams. This platform consolidates various endpoint functions into a unified operational layer, providing a comprehensive solution for IT professionals.
S. Korea 3-Year Yield Jumps to 3.108%, Hitting Highest Level Since Aug 2024

data.title: Haikou hosts "Haikou on Stage, Dancing with the World" conference to boost international performing arts, Jan 14, 2026.
data.description: Recently, the Haikou Municipal Bureau of Tourism, Culture, Radio, Television, and Sports in China hosted the "Haikou on Stage, Dancing with the World" International Performing Arts Capital Promotion Conference. This event, held in Haikou, attracted significant attention and participation. The conference aimed to promote Haikou as an international hub for performing arts, showcasing its vibrant cultural scene to the world. Key stakeholders from the performing arts industry, both domestic and international, convened to explore collaborations and opportunities. This initiative underscores Haikou's commitment to becoming a global destination for cultural exchange and creativity.

data.title: SkyDrive to sell 20 eVTOL aircraft to AeroGulf; 10 units in 2028 & 10 in 2029. Agreement reached on pricing & delivery.
data.description: Japanese company SkyDrive Inc., a prominent eVTOL aircraft manufacturer, has recently disclosed a preliminary agreement with AeroGulf Services Company LLC for the acquisition of 20 units of the "SKYDRIVE" eVTOL aircraft model SD-05. The deal sets out the terms for the purchase, including pricing and delivery schedules, with 10 units planned for acquisition in 2028 followed by an additional 10 units in 2029. This arrangement signifies a noteworthy development in the aviation industry.

data.title: YI TING Non-Woven boosts capacity for sustainable solutions, expanding in Europe and North America. Lead times cut by 30%.
data.description: In a strategic move, YI TING has expanded its operations positioning the company as a resilient supply partner. The expansion has resulted in cutting lead times by up to 30% for sustainable filtration. Located in Taoyuan, this move comes as YI TING has gained trust from world-renowned specialty coffee equipment innovators and Tier-1 roasters. The company's non-woven products are highly regarded in the industry for their quality and efficiency. This development is set to significantly impact the supply chain, with notable improvements in delivery times and sustainability practices.

data.title: Bitcoin's open interest drops 30%, suggesting potential bullish rebound amid deleveraging. Market bottoms may indicate recovery.
data.description: The open interest in Bitcoin derivatives saw a significant decline of 30% from the peak levels seen in October. This drop was driven by deleveraging, which aimed to eliminate excessive leverage in the market. In the past, such reductions in open interest have often indicated market bottoms and have been followed by a recovery in prices.

data.title: Vienna's Bitpanda eyes $5B valuation in potential 2026 Frankfurt IPO amidst growing interest in public listings for digital asset firms.
data.description: A cryptocurrency platform headquartered in Vienna is considering the possibility of a listing in Germany by 2026, as an increasing number of digital asset firms are preparing for public trading opportunities.
